CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-08-2024 Off Calendar

Petition To Approve Compromise Of Disputed Claim Minor
Off Calendar Dec-22-2023 Off Calendar

Petition To Approve Compromise Of Disputed Claim Minor
Off Calendar Dec-22-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for Plaintiff's motion for compromise of claim of Minor, Angel Lamas.

Dept. 505; Judge Maria Evangelista; Clerk: N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:00 am. and concluding at 10:33 am.

Plaintiff and Guardian Ad Litem Maria de La Cruz appearing represented by Julia Wobbe, Esq. and is placed under oath via Zoom video conference.
Claimant Angel Lamas appearing represented by Julia Wobbe, Esq., and placed under oath via Zoom video conference.
Defendant Clara Haynes not appearing.

Counsel for Plaintiff presents brief recitation of case.
Guardian ad Litem is examined by the Court.

The matter is submitted.

The Court finds the settlement is reasonable and the Guardian ad Litem confirms it is in the best interest of the claimant.

The court hereby APPROVES Plaintiff's Motion for Compromise of Claim for the settlement amount of $10,000.00 minus the following:

1. $0 for Attorney's Fees
2. $0 for Medical Expenses
3. $0 for Costs

Balance of settlement in the sum of $10,000.00 shall be transfered to a custodian for the benefitof the minor under California Uniform Transfers to Minors Act. The Custodian of the funds will be Maria de La Cruz as Guardian ad litem, Petitioner, and Mother of the minor Angel Lamas. the custodianship shall continue untijl the claimant reaches age of eighteen (18).

Proposed Order Approving Compromise of Claim or Action or Disposition of Proceeds of Judgment for Minor or Person with a Disability is signed and filed this date. (D505)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash
Off Calendar Jun-03-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Julia O. Wobbe; Tobener Ravenscroft LLP; 415-504-2165; appearing via Zoom on behalf of plaintiffs Maria de la Cruz, Angel Gabriel Lamas, Gabriel Lamas, Gabriel Lamas, Jr. and Lizbeth Lamas.
Janice Haynes; defendant in pro per; 310-529-0581; appearing via Zoom on their own behalf.

Plaintiffs' counsel contests the tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS is GRANTED. Purported sub-service at the UPS store on the first attempt is improper in light of Plaintiff's actual knowledge of other service address at the time of the service attempt. Wobbe Declaration, paragraph 4. Therefore, November 19, 2020 service is quashed. The Court notes that this issue may be moot in light of January 2020 service (POS filed on March 11, 2021) not addressed in this motion filed on March 3, 2021. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
